other_observers: Glenn Walbek; I believe I am the original finder of said species.
specific_location: The last street running east-west on the north side of town.
description: Small warbler-sized bird with a thick beak, slight hook at the tip. Dark gray hood with a sharp line of demarcation between the hood and the white throat. Bold white “spectacles” contrasted with the gray hood. Sides of breast, and flanks with a dirty yellow-green wash. Throat, center of breast, belly and undertail coverts clean white. Vent with a slight yellow tint. Tail relatively short. Back gray-green contrasting with gray hood. Wings gray with discernable edge color to the flight feathers (maybe a worn adult?). Two bold white wingbars, the lower being "bolder".Behaviors: The bird was foraging and feeding on insects it found in the Siberian Elms along the street.Call: No vocalizations were heard.Plumage: Fall adult?
